DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFFS GABRIELA FLORES, JAQUELINE FLORES, AND SAMUEL FLORES, JR. BY AND THROUGH THEIR GUARDIAN AD LITEM, RODOLFO GOMEZ MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS TO DEPOSITION QUESTIONS POSED TO DEFENDANT IRISH AMERICAN TRUCKING. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS TO DEPOSITION QUESTIONS IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. AS TO THOSE QUESTIONS WHERE DEFENDANT HAS RAISED 5TH. AMENDMENT OBJECTIONS, PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO FILE A DECLARATION TO SUPPORT THE CLAIM THAT THE MARIN DA IS NOT INVESTIGATING THIS MATTER AND DEFENDANTS' DECLARANT, DOUGLAS HORNGRAD HAS RAISED POSSIBILITY THAT DEFENDANT MAY INCRIMINATE HIMSELF BY ANSWERING THOSE QUESTIONS AS TO THE ISSUE OF DEFENDANTS TAX RETURNS, PLAINTIFF HAS OTHER AVENUES TO TEST THE EMPLOYEE INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR ISSUE AND UNTIL IT HAS SHOWN THAT THESE AVENUES HAVE NOT BEEN FRUITFUL. ALSO, THE EMPOYEE DEFENSE IS NOT TANTAMOUNT TO A WAIVER OF PRIVILEGE THE TAX ISSUE. DEFENDANT TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER.

Ntc Of Motion To Compel Answers To Deposition Questions
Off Calendar Apr-26-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFFS GABRIELA FLORES, JAQUELINE FLORES, AND SAMUEL FLORES, JR. BY AND THROUGH THEIR GUARDIAN AD LITEM, RODOLFO GOMEZ MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS TO DEPOSITION QUESTIONS POSED TO DEFENDANT IRISH AMERICAN TRUCKING.

APPEARANCES: Alan J. Chang, Esq. of Clapp Moroney Bellagamba Vucinich Beeman Scheley 650/989-5400 for x-deft & opposing party Irish American Trucking; no other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Motion to compel answers to deposition questions is denied without prejudice. As to those questions where defendant has raised 5th. amendment objections, Plaintiff has failed to file a declaration to support the claim that the Marin Da is not investigating this matter and Defendants' declarant, Douglas Horngrad has raised possibility that defendant may incriminate himself by answering those questions as to the issue of defendants tax returns, Plaintiff has other avenues to test the employee independent contractor issue and until it has shown that these avenues have not been fruitful. Also, the empoyee defense is not tantamount to a waiver of privilege the tax issue. Defendant to prepare a proposed form of order. Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Not reported
